violations.text: Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink next to 3 bay sink. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. The hand-washing sink by the 3 bay sink was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Raw shelled eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as cases of parsley in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

The following were observed in poor repair: -racks in walk-in cooler (apparent rust accumulation) -gasket on falafel machine lid (creating accumulation of black residue) -2 door reach in door handle Equipment to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

Food prep sink was observed draining slowing (bucket under air gapped discharge pipe to catch overflowing water). Plumbing system to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026
The floors in the walk-in cooler area is in poor repair (accumulated rust and exposed concrete). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2027

The following were observed in poor repair: 1. ceiling tiles in dining area (water damage) 2. 2 kitchen hood light bulbs not operational The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025
The kitchen hood vents have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Cooked Falafel was observed held at 111 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed and reheated to 213 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

Cooked chicken and shawarma were observed held at 44-64 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed and placed into refrigeration to chill during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2025

The following has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris: 1. walls in the food prep area 2. vent in dining room The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: 1. ceiling tiles in dining area (water damage) 2. laminate lined composite wood covering kitchen hood 3. kitchen hood light bulbs not operational The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. New Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. -Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the can opener during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024
